nav.channelNotConnected: Your channel isn't connected to the bot yet
nav.noModeratedChannels: You don't moderate any channels yet
nav.logoutConfirmBody: You will need to sign in with Twitch again to manage your channel.

home.title: ChatWizardBot
home.subtitle: A Twitch chat bot with moderation, mini-games, and per-channel customization. Manage settings and view stats for channels below.

about.title: About
about.intro: ChatWizardBot is a Twitch chat bot providing moderation, custom commands, counters, chat statistics, and mini-games across multiple channels.
about.supportText: For bugs, feature requests, or access issues, contact

commands.title: Available Commands
commands.subtitle: Signatures shown are defaults - each channel owner can rename them via that channel's settings page.

desc.addcommand: Create a custom command, or edit its text if it already exists.
desc.settimer: Auto-post that command's text on an interval (minimum 60s); off disables it.
desc.setpin: Toggle auto-pin for that command's sends.
desc.customcommands: List all custom command names for the channel.
desc.addcounter: Create a counter starting at 0; add mod to restrict updates to mods.
desc.addexception: Grant a user rights to update mod-restricted counters.
desc.topchatters: Top chatters leaderboard for the period.
desc.countword: How many times a word was said today.
desc.countmsg: Your message count and rank for the period.
desc.countunique: Count of unique chatters for the period.
desc.botinfo: Bot uptime and DB stats summary.
desc.addword: Track a word for word-count stats.
desc.update7tv: Re-sync the channel's 7TV emote set into the tracked-word whitelist.
desc.muteduel: Challenge chat or a user to a dice-roll duel; loser gets timed out.
desc.sovet: Get a random Dota 2 item suggestion.

useCase.addcommand: Set up a !discord or !socials command once and let chat trigger it any time.
useCase.delcommand: Retire a command that's no longer relevant.
useCase.settimer: Auto-remind viewers of a !discord link every 10 minutes without anyone typing it.
useCase.setpin: Keep an important announcement pinned whenever it's sent.
useCase.customcommands: Let viewers discover what commands a channel has set up.
useCase.triggerCustom: Everyday use of any mod-created command.
useCase.addcounter: Track deaths, wins, or any running tally live during a stream.
useCase.delcounter: Remove a counter once a challenge/event is over.
useCase.counters: Check what counters exist before creating a duplicate.
useCase.counterUpdate: Viewers or mods bump a tally as events happen on stream.
useCase.addexception: Let a trusted regular update a mod-only counter without full mod status.
useCase.remexception: Revoke that trust if it's no longer needed.
useCase.topchatters: Shout out the most active chatters at the end of a stream.
useCase.topsmiles: See which emotes the community actually uses most.
useCase.countword: Settle a chat debate about how often something was said.
useCase.countmsg: Check your own chat activity and rank.
useCase.countunique: Gauge how many different people showed up today.
useCase.botinfo: Mods checking the bot is alive and healthy mid-stream.
useCase.addword: Start tracking a new emote/word for !topsmiles stats.
useCase.remword: Stop tracking a word that's no longer relevant.
useCase.update7tv: Pick up newly added 7TV emotes without waiting for the next auto-sync.
useCase.muteduel: A fun PvP wager between two chatters, loser gets timed out.
useCase.muteaccept: Accepting a challenge someone sent you.
useCase.sovet: A lighthearted random Dota 2 item suggestion for chat.

games.title: Mini-Games
games.comingSoonSubtitle: On-site games are in the works. In the meantime, chat mini-games like !muteduel are listed on the Commands page.

channelSettings.subtitle: Changes take effect within a few seconds - no bot restart needed.
channelSettings.bannedWordsIntro: A user is timed out if they type one of these words and ping the bot with "@ChatWizardBot" in the same message.
channelSettings.timeoutReasonHint: Shown to the user as the reason for their timeout.
channelSettings.spamSignaturesIntro: A message is re-obfuscated (homoglyphs, spacing tricks, etc. normalized back to plain text) before being checked against these signatures. A match results in a permanent ban.
channelSettings.spamSignaturesExample: Example signature "subscribe to my channel ExampleChannel" also catches messages using modified/obfuscated fonts to spell it out.

commandDesc.topchatters: Leaderboard of the most active chatters for a time period.
commandDesc.topsmiles: Leaderboard of the most-used emotes for a time period.
commandDesc.countword: Reports how many times a specific word was said.
commandDesc.countmsg: Reports a user's own message count and rank.
commandDesc.countunique: Reports how many unique chatters have spoken.
commandDesc.botinfo: Shows bot uptime and database stats (mods only).
commandDesc.addword: Lets mods add or stop tracking words for word-count stats.
commandDesc.addcommand: Lets mods create, edit, and delete custom commands.
commandDesc.settimer: Lets mods set a custom command to auto-post on an interval.
commandDesc.setpin: Lets mods toggle auto-pinning a custom command's sends.
commandDesc.exception: Lets mods grant or revoke a user's right to update mod-restricted counters.
commandDesc.update7tv: Lets mods manually re-sync the channel's 7TV emote set.
commandDesc.muteduel: The mini-game where chat can challenge each other to a dice-roll duel.
commandDesc.question: The bot answers yes/no questions when pinged.
commandDesc.directmsg: The bot responds to pings that aren't a recognized command.
commandDesc.insult: Banned-word detection - times out users who trigger it (see the Banned Words page).
commandDesc.customCommandTimer: How many ordinary chat messages must pass between two auto-posted custom commands, so auto-sends don't land back-to-back.
commandDesc.counterUpdate: Lets viewers or mods bump a counter's value in chat; cooldown between updates.

channelSettings.responsesIntro: What the bot says when it's pinged directly ("@ChatWizardBot ...").
channelSettings.busyResponsesHint: A random one of these is sent when the bot is pinged but has nothing more specific to say.
channelSettings.yesNoResponsesHint: Sent when someone asks the bot a yes/no question.
channelSettings.insultModExemptHint: Sent instead of a timeout when the bot's ban-word check targets someone who's actually a moderator.
channelSettings.insufficientPermissionsHint: Sent whenever the bot is asked to do something that needs moderator status in the channel (e.g. timing out a banned-word violator) but it isn't a moderator here.
channelSettings.unsavedChangesWarning: You have unsaved changes. If you leave now, they'll be lost.

accountSettings.title: Settings
accountSettings.subtitle: Personal preferences for your account on this site.

errors.accessDeniedMessage: You don't have permission to view this page.
errors.notFoundMessage: The page or channel you're looking for doesn't exist.
errors.serverErrorMessage: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.

userDashboard.noMessages: No messages in this channel yet
userDashboard.noTrackedEmotes: This channel has no tracked 7TV emotes
userDashboard.sampled: Showing the most recent messages in this period, not all of them

channelDashboard.noTrackedEmotes: No tracked emotes yet — this channel has no 7TV set synced
channelDashboard.cloudCapNote: Emotes are excluded — they have their own board

customCommands.title: Custom commands
customCommands.subtitle: Created here or with !addcommand in chat — same commands, same bot
customCommands.name: Command
customCommands.pin: Pin to chat when posted (moderators only)
customCommands.countersHint: Use #counterName to insert a counter's current value
customCommands.saved: Saved — live in chat within a few seconds

errors.name_invalid: Only letters and digits, no spaces or symbols
errors.result_too_long: Response is too long for a Twitch message
errors.timer_invalid: Auto-post interval must be a number of seconds
errors.timer_too_short: Auto-post interval is too short (minimum 60 seconds)
errors.timer_and_pin: A command can have an auto-post timer or auto-pin, not both — Twitch allows only one pinned message per channel
